SUMMARY: NESTED | FIELD | CONSTR | METHOD DETAIL: FIELD | CONSTR | METHOD

javax.media.mscontrol
Interface MsControlFactory

public interface MsControlFactory

This factory creates MediaSessions.
It creates also the objects that are not tied to a particular MediaSession.
(The objects that have a relationship to MediaSession are created by the MediaSession itself.)
Note: The internals of this factory are implementation-dependant, and for this reason it is provided as an interface. As a result, another class is required to build it: MsControlFactory

See Also:
Driver, DriverManager

Field Summary
static java.lang.String MEDIA_SERVER_URI
          MsControlFactory property defining an URI of a media server, for example sip:ms@192.168.1.2:5060.

          Returns an array of mixer VideoLayouts that are supported by the mediaserver.

          Gets a read-only copy of this factory's properties
 

Field Detail

MEDIA_SERVER_URI

static final java.lang.String MEDIA_SERVER_URI
MsControlFactory property defining an URI of a media server, for example sip:ms@192.168.1.2:5060.

See Also:
Constant Field Values
Method Detail

getProperties

java.util.Properties getProperties()
Gets a read-only copy of this factory's properties

Returns:
the properties of this factory
See Also:
getFactoryPropertyInfo

createMediaSession

MediaSession createMediaSession()
                                throws MsControlException
Throws:
MsControlException

getMediaConfig

MediaConfig getMediaConfig(Configuration<?> configuration)
                           throws MediaConfigException
Return an instance of predefined MediaConfig, as described by the given Configuration

Parameters:
configuration - a Configuration identifying a predefined config, for example MediaGroup.PLAYER_RECORDER_SIGNALDETECTOR
Returns:
an instance of MediaConfig
Throws:
MediaConfigException
See Also:
getMediaConfig(Reader)

getMediaConfig

MediaConfig getMediaConfig(java.io.Reader xmlDef)
                           throws MediaConfigException
Create an instance of MediaConfig, from an xml byte stream (for example a file, or a String),
Example: 
Reader xmlDoc = new StringReader("<?xml .... >");
 MediaConfig myNCConfig = myMediaSessionFactory.getMediaConfig(xmlDoc);
The schema for the xml document is provided by the media-server vendor.

Parameters:
xmlDef - A Reader able to provide an xml byte stream
Returns:
an instance of a class derived from MediaConfig, with the expected type
Throws:
MediaConfigException
See Also:
getMediaConfig(Configuration)

createParameters

Parameters createParameters()
Create an empty Parameters map

createVideoLayout

VideoLayout createVideoLayout(java.lang.String mimeType,
                              java.io.Reader xmlDef)
                              throws MediaConfigException
Factory method for creating a instance of VideoLayout.

Parameters:
mimeType - e.g., "application/smil+xml"
xmlDef - A byte stream providing an xml definition for the layout
Throws:
MediaConfigException

getPresetLayouts

VideoLayout[] getPresetLayouts(int numberOfLiveRegions)
                               throws MediaConfigException
Returns an array of mixer VideoLayouts that are supported by the mediaserver.
Each entry in the array may differ from the previous by the size of the regions, decoration, etc.
Simple layouts should have low indexes.
Some media servers may support only those layouts.
Some media servers may support only minor modifications to those layouts: for example, structural modifications disallowed (only attributes values can be changed).
The array may have a null size.
Note: This is a helper method for implementations or media servers that cannot provide full support of an xml format.

Parameters:
numberOfLiveRegions - The number of regions, in the rendered image, that can display a live stream (for instance, a NetworkConnection, or a MediaGroup playing a clip).
Throws:
MediaConfigException

getPresetLayout

VideoLayout getPresetLayout(java.lang.String type)
                            throws MediaConfigException
Returns a VideoLayout (supported by the mediaserver), with the characteristics as defined by type

Parameters:
type - A string to further describe the desired layout, see VideoLayout#getType().
Throws:
MediaConfigException
See Also:
getPresetLayouts(int)

getMediaObject

MediaObject getMediaObject(java.net.URI mediaObjectURI)
Search for a MediaObject with the given URI.
The search is performed in the child objects of this factory (recursively).

Returns:
a MediaObject with the given URI, or null if not found.
